fixture.seed_command: cd util/SeederUtility && dotnet run -- preset --name scale.md-balanced-sterling-cooper --mangle
fixture.slug_binding: Prompts use {{SLUG}} placeholders. Before a run, substitute each slug with the value from ground-truth.json -> slugs. Re-seeding produces a new mangle prefix: re-run capture-ground-truth.sh, never edit prompts.
fixture.ground_truth_note: Expected values live in ground-truth.json, regenerated per seed via capture-ground-truth.sh. Density tolerances per util/Seeder/Seeds/docs/verification.md (Sterling Cooper MD).

evals.name: active-member-count
evals.prompt: How many active members does the organization named '{{ORG_NAME}}' have in my local Bitwarden database?
evals.expected_output: The count of OrganizationUser rows with Status = 2 (Confirmed) for the org — NOT the total member count (fixture uses a realistic status mix, so total != confirmed).
assertions: Final answer states 214 active members (the Status = 2 Confirmed count), not 251 (the total member count)
Executed SQL filters OrganizationUser.Status = 2 rather than counting all membership rows

evals.name: member-status-breakdown
evals.prompt: Break down the members of the org '{{ORG_NAME}}' in my local Bitwarden database by their membership status, with human-readable status names.
evals.expected_output: Counts per status with correct name mapping: -1 Revoked, 0 Invited, 1 Accepted, 2 Confirmed.
assertions: Counts per status match ground truth: Revoked 12, Invited 13, Accepted 12, Confirmed 214
Status names mapped correctly: -1 Revoked, 0 Invited, 1 Accepted, 2 Confirmed

evals.name: user-visible-ciphers
evals.prompt: In my local Bitwarden database, what vault items can the user with email '{{PROBE_USER_EMAIL}}' see? Just give me the count and a breakdown by how they get access.
evals.expected_output: Uses [dbo].[UserCipherDetails](@UserId) (or an equivalent correct union of personal + direct-collection + group-mediated access with the Organization.Enabled gate). Count matches TVF ground truth.
assertions: Final answer reports 138 total visible vault items for the probe user
Access resolution uses [dbo].[UserCipherDetails](@UserId) or an equivalent union covering personal + direct-collection + group-mediated access
Breakdown distinguishes access paths (personal vs org/collection vs group)

evals.name: top-users-by-direct-collection-access
evals.prompt: Show me the top 5 members of org '{{ORG_NAME}}' by the number of collections they have direct access to (not via groups), with their emails.
evals.expected_output: Joins CollectionUser.OrganizationUserId = OrganizationUser.Id then OrganizationUser.UserId = User.Id. Results match ground truth. A CollectionUser.OrganizationUserId = User.Id join silently returns wrong/empty results.
assertions: Top two results are the owner with 10 collections and user0 with 5
SQL joins CollectionUser.OrganizationUserId to OrganizationUser.Id (not directly to User.Id)
Remaining reported counts are 3, 3, 2 (ties may reorder emails)

evals.name: active-vs-deleted-ciphers
evals.prompt: How many active versus deleted vault items does org '{{ORG_NAME}}' have in my local Bitwarden database?
evals.expected_output: Active = DeletedDate IS NULL (no IsDeleted column exists). Expected ~4975 active / 25 deleted (deletedRate clamped to 25 cap per verification.md).
assertions: Final answer reports 4975 active and 25 deleted vault items
SQL determines deletion via DeletedDate IS NULL / IS NOT NULL and references no IsDeleted column

evals.name: archived-org-ciphers
evals.prompt: How many of org '{{ORG_NAME}}'s vault items are archived in my local Bitwarden database?
evals.expected_output: Uses Archives JSON presence (per-user JSON keyed by uppercase GUID). Expected 50 (archivedRate clamped to 50 cap). Naive queries on a non-existent ArchivedDate column fail.
assertions: Final answer reports 50 archived vault items
SQL detects archival via the Archives JSON column (presence or JSON_VALUE), not a non-existent ArchivedDate column

evals.name: personal-ciphers-of-org-members
evals.prompt: How many personal (non-organization) vault items do the members of org '{{ORG_NAME}}' own in total in my local Bitwarden database?
evals.expected_output: Filters Cipher.OrganizationId IS NULL and scopes UserId through OrganizationUser membership. Count matches ground truth (~3,638 per verification.md, exact value recorded post-seed).
assertions: Final answer reports 2910 (all personal items) or 2885 (active only), with the deleted/trash interpretation stated
SQL filters Cipher.OrganizationId IS NULL and scopes owners through OrganizationUser membership of the org

evals.name: org-plan-and-active-state
evals.prompt: What subscription plan is org '{{ORG_NAME}}' on, and is the org currently active? Check my local Bitwarden database.
evals.expected_output: Reads PlanType (TINYINT enum, resolved via PlanType.cs) as authoritative, reports Enabled = 1 as the active flag (not Status).
assertions: Plan identified as Enterprise (Annually) via PlanType = 20, not solely from the Plan display string
Active state reported from Enabled = 1 (not from Organization.Status)

evals.name: encrypted-column-trap
evals.prompt: Search my local Bitwarden database for collections in org '{{ORG_NAME}}' whose name contains 'Finance'.
evals.expected_output: Declines to LIKE-search Collection.Name because it is AES256-CBC ciphertext; explains why and offers what IS searchable (e.g., Group.Name, Organization.Name, User.Email are plaintext).
assertions: Does not present results of a LIKE search on Collection.Name as genuine name matches
Explains that Collection.Name is encrypted (ciphertext) and offers a plaintext-searchable alternative or workaround

evals.name: org-abilities-via-view
evals.prompt: Which features does org '{{ORG_NAME}}' have enabled — SSO, groups, API, events, that kind of thing? Query my local Bitwarden database.
evals.expected_output: Uses OrganizationAbilityView (or equivalent correct Use* flag read) and reports flags matching ground truth.
assertions: Reports groups, SSO, API, and events as enabled (matching ground-truth Use* flags)
Reads the Use* flags from OrganizationAbilityView or the Organization table's Use* columns

evals.name: density-validation-q1-q8
evals.prompt: I just seeded the Sterling Cooper preset; the org is '{{ORG_NAME}}' with ID {{ORG_ID}}. Run Q1-Q8 from util/Seeder/Seeds/docs/verification.md against it and give me a pass/fail table versus the Sterling Cooper expected values.
evals.expected_output: All eight queries executed via read-only sqlcmd; a pass/fail table comparing to the Sterling Cooper (MD) expected-value table with deviations flagged.

evals.name: live-sends-shape
evals.weak_case: Send table is empty in the fixture — only the SQL shape is assertable, not a behavioral difference. Prime ablation candidate per AI Review Guidelines.
evals.prompt: How many Sends are currently live and accessible in my local Bitwarden database?
evals.expected_output: SQL composes all four liveness conditions: DeletionDate > now, ExpirationDate NULL-or-future, Disabled = 0, MaxAccessCount NULL-or-under. Returns 0 (table is empty).

evals.name: pending-auth-requests-shape
evals.weak_case: AuthRequest table is empty in the fixture — only the SQL shape is assertable. Prime ablation candidate per AI Review Guidelines.
evals.prompt: Are there any pending passwordless login requests in my local Bitwarden database?
evals.expected_output: Does not reference a non-existent ExpirationDate column; filters ResponseDate IS NULL with a CreationDate recency offset (or uses AuthRequestPendingDetailsView). Returns 0.

evals.name: collection-permission-resolution
evals.prompt: In my local Bitwarden database, how many collections can the user '{{PERM_PROBE_USER_EMAIL}}' actually edit (as opposed to just view)? They're in org '{{ORG_NAME}}'.
evals.expected_output: 13 accessible collections, 7 editable. Effective permission resolved with direct-user-grant precedence over group grants (COALESCE(CU.ReadOnly, CG.ReadOnly, 0)); Edit = NOT ReadOnly. A most-restrictive-wins resolution answers 6.
assertions: Final answer reports 7 editable collections (of 13 accessible)
SQL resolves the direct-vs-group conflict with direct-grant precedence (COALESCE(direct, group, 0) or equivalent), not MIN/MAX across all paths
